title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores whether AI systems can be socially engineered to leak credentials and discusses a self-replicating AI worm from University of Toronto. The episode examines whether AI agents are more gullible than humans and reviews findings from the Sophos State of Identity Security 2026 report on nonhuman identity breaches.
writeup: This IBM Technology episode, from the IBM Security Intelligence podcast, examines whether AI systems can be socially engineered into leaking credentials and whether AI agents prove more gullible than humans. It covers a self-replicating AI worm developed at the University of Toronto and reviews findings from the Sophos State of Identity Security 2026 report on breaches tied to nonhuman identities. The discussion connects prompt injection and credential management to the growing attack surface created by autonomous agents.
